title: Psidium acidum

taxonomy: Psidium acidum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a member of the genus Psidium, which includes various species of guavas. The plant's classification is further supported by its characteristics and botanical features. In the context of taxonomy, Psidium acidum is considered a type of fruit-bearing tree or shrub. Its classification is an essential aspect of understanding the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other species.
uses: Psidium acidum, also known as guava, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. side_effects: Psidium acidum, also known as the guava, is generally considered safe when consumed in moderate amounts. However, excessive consumption of guava may cause stomach upset, diarrhea, and nausea in some individuals. Additionally, people with certain medical conditions, such as diabetes, should be cautious when consuming guava due to its high sugar content. Furthermore, guava seeds and leaves may cause allergic reactions in some people, leading to symptoms like itching, redness, and swelling. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before consuming guava, especially if you have any pre-existing medical conditions.
